title: nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. hirtellum
intro: Nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. hirtellum, also known as the 'false garlic', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. hirtellum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. hirtellum can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat various skin conditions and infections.
